summary: smoke test of the orchestrate substrate end-to-end

tasks.name: marker
tasks.scopedGoal: Smoke-test the orchestrate substrate by appending a single new line at the end of `docs/smoke-test.md` (create the file if it does not exist; if it already exists, only append, do not rewrite). The new line must be exactly: `- <ISO-8601 UTC timestamp> orchestrate substrate ran end-to-end (worker `marker`)`. Then commit with message `smoke-test: marker reached worker` and push the branch. Do nothing else: no refactors, no other files, no merges, no PR. The point is to exercise spawn -> commit -> push -> structured handoff, not to ship product code.
acceptance: docs/smoke-test.md exists on the worker branch
docs/smoke-test.md ends with exactly one new line of the form '- <ISO-8601 UTC timestamp> orchestrate substrate ran end-to-end (worker `marker`)'
Worker handoff includes Status, Branch, What I did, Measurements, Verification, Notes, Suggested follow-ups sections
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Check out the worker's pushed branch.

## Automated
- `tail -n 1 docs/smoke-test.md` matches `^- \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T.* orchestrate substrate ran end-to-end \(worker \`marker\`\)$`.
- `git log -n 1 --pretty=%s` is `smoke-test: marker reached worker`.

## Manual
- (none)

## Gotchas
- Worker must append a single new line; pre-existing content must be preserved. The verifier confirms with `tail -n 1`.

tasks.name: verify-marker
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the `marker` worker actually ran end-to-end. Check out its branch, confirm the last line of `docs/smoke-test.md` matches the required pattern, and confirm the most recent commit is `smoke-test: marker reached worker`. Produce the structured verifier handoff (## Verification + ## Target + ## Branch + ## Execution + ## Findings + ## Notes & suggestions). Do not modify any files. The verifier verdict should be `unit-test-verified` if both checks pass; `verifier-failed` otherwise.
acceptance: Verifier handoff contains a `## Verification` value of `unit-test-verified` or `verifier-failed`
Verifier handoff lists each acceptance criterion of `marker` with met/not-met evidence
